steps.title: Use the graph shape
steps.content: The scatterplot rises and then falls, so the best quadratic model should open downward. That means the coefficient of $x^2$ must be negative.

steps.title: Use the intercept size
steps.content: The data values are in the hundreds, so the y-intercept should be positive and near $745$. This rules out the model with negative intercept $-745.73$.

steps.title: Select the remaining model
steps.content:
The remaining equation opens downward and has the appropriate positive intercept:
$$y=-1.674x^2+19.76x+745.73$$
The answer is D.
